authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — Grammar assessment criteria for healthcare English

criteria.title: Tense Accuracy
criteria.description: Correct tense choice for clinical narratives: past simple for completed events, present perfect for ongoing, present simple for standing facts.

criteria.title: Voice & Agency
criteria.description: Appropriate use of passive voice for clinical actions and admissions, without over-use.

criteria.title: Articles & Countability
criteria.description: Correct article usage for diseases (zero article), countable symptoms ('a'), and investigations with vowel sounds ('an ECG').

criteria.title: Subject–Verb Agreement
criteria.description: Correct handling of proximity rule ('or'), reverse rule ('along with'), collective nouns, and medical plurals.

criteria.title: Professional Register
criteria.description: No contractions, no 'the patient' as running label, no colloquial terms, lowercase conditions, no patient labels.

criteria.title: Linkers & Punctuation
criteria.description: Correct punctuation of 'however', 'therefore', 'in addition', sparing use (≤2 per paragraph), sensible sentence length (15–25 words).

criteria.title: Prepositions & Time Markers
criteria.description: Treatment FOR not FROM; correct 'on/in/at' usage; elapsed-time phrasing; hyphenated duration adjectives.

criteria.title: Clinical Numeracy & Dose Language
criteria.description: Latin abbreviations translated, values paired with units, dates formatted correctly, no 'PO' for oral route.

notes: These criteria feed the Language and Organisation & Layout bands of the OET Writing rubric and map to IELTS Grammatical Range & Accuracy / PTE Grammar trait. Lessons authored against this rulebook must cite the relevant criterion in their sourceProvenance.
